title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What makes a capital guarantee plan a long-term saving?
Are you someone looking to grow your money and yet protect what you invest? Are you planning to safeguard your family’s future as well? A capital guarantee plan[1] could be a wise choice. Combining the advantages of life insurance and a market-linked investment, this tax-saving pick can help you secure your capital while growing your money. But before you choose to invest, it’s crucial to understand what makes a capital guarantee plan a long-term saving.
What makes a capital guarantee plan a long-term saving?
To figure it out, let us begin with what a capital guarantee plan is[1]. Designed to cater mostly to risk-averse investors, a capital guarantee plan is a hybrid financial instrument that invests your money in market-linked funds to earn returns but also, refunds all the premiums paid upon maturity of the policy. Hence, you get back what you invested along with returns, if any. Alongside, a capital guarantee plan also pays out a death benefit to the nominee in an unforeseen event. It puts your money in a strategic mix of equities, bonds, and other funds, a long-term investment is needed to earn good returns. What makes it stand out is that your capital remains 100% secure as you get back whatever you’ve invested along with additional returns. And this very nature is what makes capital guarantee plans a long-term saving.
Benefits and features of a capital guarantee plan
A capital guarantee solution plan may seem lucrative as it lets you grow your wealth while ensuring a guaranteed return of your investment. But knowing its features and benefits can help in making an informed choice. Here’s what you need to know[2]:
A capital guarantee plan is a blend of life insurance with maturity benefits and a unit-linked insurance plan (ULIP). A part of your premium forms the insurance component; the other is invested in market-linked instruments.
Usually 50-60% of the invested funds go to guaranteed return products like fixed income assets, which protect the capital. Rest is put into equities, bonds, and hybrid funds seeking growth in returns.
At the end of the policy term, the return of capital is ensured by the guaranteed return component, while the growth of money, if any, is contributed by the ULIP component.
These plans require you to stay invested for the long term[3] to fetch higher returns from equities. In the end, you not only get to grow your wealth but get back the paid premiums too. That’s what makes capital guarantee plans a long-term saving tool, where you earn more than a traditional savings plan.
Here the investment strategies are planned and executed by experienced fund managers. They may balance the potential losses and gains by putting your funds more into bonds, and less into equities[3].
Entitled to get back all the premiums paid, you can stay safe from any kind of uncertainties and volatility of the market, ensuring peace of mind.
A capital guarantee plan comes with tax benefits under the provisions of sections 80C and 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961. The tax benefits under Section 80C may be availed through the old tax regime.
Who can opt for a capital guarantee plan?
Since the return of the total premiums paid is guaranteed, a capital guarantee plan may be a good pick for risk-averse investors who want to grow their wealth and are yet sceptical about losing money. It may also be a worthy pick for those who want a one-stop solution for all their investment needs while saving on taxes. The eligibility criteria for investing in capital guarantee plans varies from product to product, depending on age of the investor, policy term, premium-paying term, minimum investment, etc. However, usually the minimum age limit to purchase a capital guarantee plan in India is 18 years.
Conclusion
By now you might be aware of the advantages of capital guarantee plan and its long-term saving benefits. Nonetheless, nothing beats a careful reading of the plan details to know in exact what will happen to your hard-earned money and how you are going to benefit from the investment. Also, it’s useful to know how it aligns with the current tax laws of the country. This may save you from unnecessary hassles.
FAQs
What happens to my capital guarantee plan if the insurer defaults?
Bankruptcy or default can happen to any company. Insurers are no exception. To save policyholders from potential monetary losses arising out of defaults by the insurer, IRDAI protects their interests by setting mandates for insurance companies[4].
Can I add riders or additional coverage to my capital guarantee plan?
Features and benefits of a capital guarantee plan vary from company to company. Since these plans have a life insurance component, some insurers offer optional riders like accident cover, critical or terminal illness coverage, waiver of premium in disability due to accident or diseases, etc. If you decide to purchase a capital guarantee plan, it may be worthwhile to check the available set of riders to enhance its coverage.
Is a capital guarantee plan suitable for all investors?
The guaranteed return is what makes a capital guarantee plan a long-term saving, fetching returns from market-linked funds alongside. Since the capital stays protected and getting it back is ensured at maturity, this plan may be ideal for the investors with no risk appetite. But the returns, on the other hand, are moderate [5]due to a conservative approach to investment. Thus, those who prefer a high-risk, high-gain investment profile may not find the capital guarantee plan to be a suitable choice.
How does a capital guarantee plan differ from traditional savings accounts?
The capital guarantee plan may help your income grow by earning returns from the investments made in market-linked funds[2]. Alongside, there’s a safety net for your capital as you get a guaranteed return of the premiums paid upon maturity of the policy. Unlike traditional savings instruments, which let you earn interest at a fixed rate, returns from a capital guarantee plan depend on the volatility of the market and the reaction of your chosen funds to it.
Reference:
full_pdf_text:
What makes a capital guarantee plan a long-term saving?
Are you someone looking to grow your money and yet protect what you invest? Are you planning to safeguard your family’s future as well? A capital guarantee plan[1] could be a wise choice. Combining the advantages of life insurance and a market-linked investment, this tax-saving pick can help you secure your capital while growing your money. But before you choose to invest, it’s crucial to understand what makes a capital guarantee plan a long-term saving.
What makes a capital guarantee plan a long-term saving?
To figure it out, let us begin with what a capital guarantee plan is. Designed to cater mostly to risk-averse investors, a capital guarantee plan is a hybrid financial instrument that invests your money in market-linked funds to earn returns but also, refunds all the premiums paid upon maturity of the policy.1 Hence, you get back what you invested along with returns, if any. Alongside, a capital guarantee plan also pays out a death benefit to the nominee in an unforeseen event. It puts your money in a strategic mix of equities, bonds, and other funds, as long-term investment is needed to earn good returns. What makes it stand out is that your capital remains 100% secure as you get back whatever you’ve invested along with any market- linked returns you may have earned. And this very nature is what makes capital guarantee plans a long-term saving.
Benefits and features of a capital guarantee plan
A capital guarantee solution plan may seem lucrative as it lets you grow your wealth while ensuring a guaranteed* return of your investment. But knowing its features and benefits can help in making an informed choice. Here’s what you need to know[2]:
 A capital guarantee plan is a blend of life insurance with maturity benefits and a unit-linked insurance plan (ULIP). A part of your premium forms the insurance component; the other is invested in market-linked instruments.
 Usually 50-60% of the invested funds go to guaranteed return products like fixed income assets, which protect the capital. Rest is put into equities, bonds, and hybrid funds seeking growth in returns.
 At the end of the policy term, the return of capital is ensured by the guaranteed* return component, while the growth of money, if any, is contributed by the ULIP component i.e the additional growth of your money is dependent on market performance.
 Here the investment strategies are planned and executed by experienced fund managers. They may balance the potential losses and gains by putting your funds more into bonds, and less into equities[3].
 Entitled to get back all the premiums paid, subject to policy terms & conditions, you can stay safe from any kind of uncertainties and volatility of the market, ensuring peace of mind.
 A capital guarantee plan comes with tax benefits under the provisions of sections 80C and 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961. The tax benefits under Section 80C may be availed through the old tax regime.
Who can opt for a capital guarantee plan?
Since the return of the total premiums paid is guaranteed*, a capital guarantee plan may be a good pick for risk-averse investors who want to grow their wealth and are yet sceptical about losing money. It may also be a worthy pick for those who want a one-stop solution for all their investment needs while saving on taxes. The eligibility criteria for investing in capital guarantee plans varies from product to product, depending on age of the investor, policy term, premium- paying term, minimum investment, etc.
Conclusion
By now you might be aware of the advantages of capital guarantee plan and its long-term saving benefits. Nonetheless, nothing beats a careful reading of the plan details to know in exact what will happen to your hard-earned money and how you are going to benefit from the investment. Also, it’s useful to know how it aligns with the current tax laws of the country. This may save you from unnecessary hassles.
FAQs
 Can I add riders or additional coverage to my capital guarantee plan?
Features and benefits of a capital guarantee plan vary from company to company. Since these plans have a life insurance component, some insurers offer optional riders like accident death benefit rider, critical illness rider, waiver of premium in disability due to accident or diseases, etc. If you decide to purchase a capital guarantee plan, it may be worthwhile to check the available set of riders to enhance its coverage.
 Is a capital guarantee plan suitable for all investors?
The guaranteed* return is what makes a capital guarantee plan a good option for long-term saving, fetching returns from market-linked funds alongside. Since the capital stays protected and getting it back is ensured at maturity, this plan may be ideal for the investors with lower risk appetite. But the returns, on the other hand, are moderate4 due to a conservative approach to investment. Thus, those who prefer a high-risk, high-gain investment profile may not find the capital guarantee plan to be a suitable choice.
 How does a capital guarantee plan differ from traditional savings accounts?
The capital guarantee plan may help your income grow by earning returns from the investments made in market-linked funds[2]. Alongside, there’s a safety net for your capital as you get a guaranteed* return of the premiums paid upon maturity of the policy. Unlike traditional savings instruments, which let you earn interest at a fixed rate, capital guarantee plan guarantees the amount you invested regardless of the market performance. Here your investment remains safe and additional amount earned, if any, would be subjected to market risks.
Reference:
[1] https://www.nationalheraldindia.com/advertorial/how-capital-guarantee-solutions-offer-safe- investment-with-growth-potential
[2] https://www.wintwealth.com/blog/capital-guarantee-plans-is-capital-guarantee-solution-a-good- bet/
[3] https://www.investopedia.com/terms/c/capitalguaranteefund.asp [4]https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/wealth/insure/how-irdai-protects-policyholders-from- crises-at-insurance-companies/articleshow/72304257.cms?from=mdr Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy.
* Conditions Apply – The Guaranteed benefits are dependent on policy term, premium payment term availed along with other variable factors. For more details please refer to sales brochure.
